title: Unrecognized server shape (nested server.url) is UNKNOWN, never SAFE

origin.description: Minimised from a real committed .cursor/mcp.json found in a public GitHub repo during the 0.3.0-rc.0 feedback window (RC-B04). The endpoint is nested one level deeper than the recognized shape — mcpServers.<name>.server.url instead of mcpServers.<name>.url — so the parser cannot resolve a runtime. The original host was masked to an example domain; no secrets were present.

humanExpectation.rationale: CallLint could not resolve this server into a recognized runtime (no url or command at the recognized depth), so its source is unverifiable. The honest verdict is UNKNOWN. Returning SAFE here — as the engine did before ADR 0010 — is a dangerous false-SAFE: a config the tool understood the least would have received the safest verdict.
knownLimitations: CallLint does not yet normalize aliased/nested url shapes into a recognized remote. Until it does, such shapes are UNKNOWN rather than a recognized remote with supply.unknown-remote. The safe-direction behaviour (UNKNOWN, never SAFE) is the contract.
